issue_title: Add regression test for Lindley default-param AIC/BIC bug
issue_body:
### Goal
Prevent silent regression of information criteria (AIC/BIC) computation when the Lindley distribution uses its default parameter value. The constructor previously used `arguments.length` to infer parameter count, which silently dropped the parameter penalty because default-filled parameters are invisible to `arguments.length`.

### Scope
- `test/dist-cases-continuous.js` — verify that the Lindley `cases` entry exercises the default-constructor path, or add a regression case
- `test/dist.js` — optionally add a targeted assertion to verify that `new ran.dist.Lindley().aic(sample)` matches `new ran.dist.Lindley(1).aic(sample)`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `new ran.dist.Lindley()` constructs without error and its `this.k` equals `1`
- [ ] `new ran.dist.Lindley().aic(data)` returns the same value as `new ran.dist.Lindley(1).aic(data)` for a non-trivial data array
- [ ] `new ran.dist.Lindley().bic(data)` likewise matches `new ran.dist.Lindley(1).bic(data)`
- [ ] `npm test` passes
- [ ] Production-code diff under 400 lines (excluding tests). If exceeded, decompose.
- [ ] PR is independently revertable without breaking main
- [ ] Prerequisites (new special functions, algorithms) filed as separate issues and merged first

### Out of Scope
- Changing the documented default value of `theta` (JSDoc `Default value is 1` is preserved)
- Auditing multi-parameter distributions for the same pattern (separate concern)
